web.title: What's the difference between a midwife and a Doula? | BabyCenter
web.description: Basically a doula is a support person who is trained or experienced in birth support. It's optional. You for sure need either an OB or midwife.

web.title: Doula vs Midwife vs Solo - Community | BabyCenter
web.description: Midwives are responsible for your medical care, a doula provides support through your labor (they don't make any medical decisions with you).
